text:
DISCHARGE SUMMARY

PRESENTING COMPLAINT:
Central crushing chest pain radiating to the left arm. Duration approximately 3 hours prior to arrival.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- Type 2 diabetes mellitus with nephropathy - on metformin
- Hypertension - on amlodipine
- Hypercholesterolaemia - on atorvastatin
Ex-smoker (40 pack-years, quit 2015)

INVESTIGATIONS:
ECG: ST elevation in anterior leads (4mm)
Troponin I: 3.2 ng/mL (reference < 0.04)
Echo: Anterior wall hypokinesis, EF 42%

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. Anterior acute transmural myocardial infarction - confirmed on ECG and troponin rise.
   Plan: Emergency PCI performed to left anterior descending artery (LAD). Drug-eluting stent inserted. Dual antiplatelet therapy commenced (aspirin + clopidogrel).

2. Type 2 diabetes mellitus with nephropathy.
   Plan: Continue metformin. Renal follow-up arranged. Lifestyle advice provided.

3. Acute kidney injury - creatinine peaked at 178 umol/L post-procedure, likely contrast nephropathy.
   Plan: IV hydration. Creatinine improving at discharge (145 umol/L).

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
Aspirin 75mg daily, Clopidogrel 75mg daily, Ramipril 5mg daily, Atorvastatin 80mg nocte, Bisoprolol 2.5mg daily

FOLLOW UP:
Cardiology outpatients in 6 weeks. Cardiac rehabilitation referral made.

DISCHARGE SUMMARY

PRESENTING COMPLAINT:
Sudden onset chest pain 7/10 severity, onset at rest. No radiation. Associated diaphoresis.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- Non-smoker

INVESTIGATIONS:
ECG: ST elevation in inferior leads II, III, aVF (3mm)
Troponin I: 5.8 ng/mL

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. Inferior acute transmural myocardial infarction - confirmed on ECG and troponin.
   Plan: Emergency PCI to right coronary artery (RCA). Stent inserted. Dual antiplatelet therapy.

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
Aspirin 75mg daily, Ticagrelor 90mg BD, Ramipril 2.5mg daily, Atorvastatin 80mg nocte

FOLLOW UP:
Cardiology outpatients in 4 weeks.

DISCHARGE SUMMARY

PRESENTING COMPLAINT:
Progressive dyspnoea over 7 days. Orthopnoea. Paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea. Unable to lie flat. Bilateral ankle swelling.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- Ischaemic cardiomyopathy - known reduced EF on previous echo
- Atrial fibrillation - on apixaban
- Hypertension

INVESTIGATIONS:
CXR: Cardiomegaly, bilateral pleural effusions, upper lobe diversion
BNP: 3400 pg/mL
Echo: EF 32% (severely reduced), no significant valve disease

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. Acute-on-chronic heart failure (HFrEF, EF 32%) - decompensated presentation. BNP markedly elevated.
   Plan: IV furosemide 80mg daily. Fluid restrict to 1.5L/day. Daily weights. Optimise treatment and arrange follow-up.

2. Atrial fibrillation - rate 110 bpm on admission, now rate-controlled at 72 bpm.
   Plan: Continue anticoagulation (apixaban). Rate control with bisoprolol.

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
Furosemide 80mg daily, Ramipril 5mg BD, Bisoprolol 5mg daily, Eplerenone 25mg daily, Apixaban 5mg BD

FOLLOW UP:
Heart failure clinic in 2 weeks.
